commit.message:
Use _byte_to_char for separators instead of store8

The my_sep/my_row_sep/my_null_marker functions used make_buffer+store8
to create 1-byte separator strings. On ARM64 with Novus V0.1.3,
back-to-back make_buffer+store8 calls can corrupt each other's buffers
due to register/memory issues.

Using _byte_to_char returns string literals from the data section,
avoiding any runtime memory allocation or store8 calls.

Requires novus-std v1.1.8+ which added bytes 1-8 to _byte_to_char.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
network: Load-aware PeerExchange for connection distribution

Foundation for balanced mesh topology:

- Add PeerExchangeEntry { address, connection_count, is_masternode }
  and PeerExchange(Vec<PeerExchangeEntry>) message type
- Track peer connection load in PeerConnectionRegistry (peer_load DashMap)
  with update_peer_load() / get_peer_load() / get_peers_by_load() helpers
- GetPeers now responds with load-sorted PeerExchange instead of a plain
  IP list so peers can steer new connections toward underloaded nodes
- Handle incoming PeerExchange: store load data and add peer candidates
- Randomize Phase 1 masternode selection (shuffle before take(N)) so
  every node picks a different subset at startup instead of always
  hammering the same first N masternodes
- Sort Phase 3 regular peer candidates by ascending known load
- Redirect overloaded inbound connections: after handshake, if inbound
  count exceeds 70 % of MAX_INBOUND and we have 3+ alternatives, send
  PeerExchange redirect and close (never below MIN_CONNECTIONS=8)

commit.message:
Integrate cargo-delta for selective CI validation (#131)

## Summary

Integrates [cargo-delta](https://github.com/tekian/cargo-delta) to
detect which packages are impacted by changes in a PR, so CI only
validates affected packages instead of the entire workspace.

## Changes

- **delta.toml** - configuration with trip wire patterns for
workspace-wide files (Cargo.toml, Cargo.lock, rust-toolchain.toml,
clippy.toml, workflow files, justfiles, etc.) and `mermaid` as a custom
include macro.
- **validation.yml** - adds a `delta` job that runs first on PRs,
analyzes baseline vs current branch, and outputs affected packages. All
downstream jobs depend on it and scope work via `package=` variable.
Push to main acts as a full workspace backstop.
- **justfile** - `target_package` now supports space-separated package
names for multi-package scoping.
- **justfiles/just_delta.just** - new recipes: `delta`,
`delta-validate`, `delta-validate-extra` for local delta workflows.
- **justfiles/just_testing.just** - `test-docs` and `run-examples`
updated to handle multi-package lists.
- **justfiles/just_quality.just** - `hack` recipe now uses
`target_package` for scoping; `coverage-package` supports multi-package.
- **justfiles/just_setup.just** - added `cargo-delta` to install-tools.
- **DEVELOPMENT.md** - documents the delta workflow for local and CI
use.

## How it works

1. **PR builds:** Delta job compares PR branch against `origin/main`,
identifies affected packages (including transitive dependents), and
downstream jobs only validate those packages.
2. **Push to main:** Backstop build validates the full workspace (no
delta).
3. **Trip wires:** Changes to workspace-wide config files trigger full
validation.
4. **Local use:** `just delta`, `just delta-validate`, `just
delta-validate-extra` for developers.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: double server-start and _ProgressTqdm missing get_lock()

Double server start:
- get_or_start_server() now does an HTTP health check against the
  default URL BEFORE checking the PID file.  The launcher process
  exits after daemonising so its PID was always 'dead', causing every
  second get_client() call to try starting a new server.

_ProgressTqdm tqdm error:
- _ProgressTqdm now subclasses tqdm.tqdm instead of being a bare
  shim, so huggingface_hub's thread pool can call get_lock() on it.
- Overrides only update() to forward file-count progress to the job
  callback; all other tqdm machinery is inherited.

commit.message:
Use grapheme iteration in DrawHotString (#4801)

* Use grapheme iteration in DrawHotString

DrawHotString iterated by rune via EnumerateRunes and rendered each
rune individually with AddRune. This breaks grapheme clusters:
combining marks sent separately via AddRune fail to compose with
their base character (e.g. e + combining acute renders as two
separate characters instead of 'é').

Replace with GraphemeHelper.GetGraphemes iteration and AddStr, which
preserves grapheme cluster integrity. The hotkey specifier check is
unchanged — it only matches single-character graphemes.

Added tests:
- DrawHotString_CombiningMarks: 3 cases verifying combining marks
  compose correctly (acute accent, tilde, embedded in word)
- DrawHotString_HotkeyWithCombiningMarks: hotkey + combining marks

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Move tests to UnitTestsParallelizable

Move DrawHotString grapheme tests from UnitTests to
UnitTestsParallelizable per review feedback. Adapt tests to use
CreateTestDriver() pattern instead of [SetupFakeApplication].

commit.message:
fix: correct field name mismatches between extension and webview messages

The webview message handlers were reading wrong field names from extension
messages, causing most handlers to operate on undefined values:

- agentCreated: read message.id/name/role instead of message.agent.id/name/role
  This created a ghost character with undefined id on every session start
- agentClosed/agentStatus/agentToolStart/agentToolDone/agentToolsClear/
  agentToolPermission/agentToolPermissionClear: all read message.id instead
  of message.agentId — status updates never reached the correct character
- agentToolStart: read message.toolId instead of message.tool
- floorTilesLoaded/wallTilesLoaded: read message.sprites instead of message.tiles

Also fixed timing issue where layoutReadyRef.current wasn't set synchronously
in the layoutLoaded handler, potentially causing existingAgents to buffer
agents that never get drained.

Added visual diagnostics (character count, position markers) to help debug
the remaining character sprite visibility issue.

commit.message:
Use string.GetColumns() for display width (#4799)

* Use string.GetColumns() for display width

Replace EnumerateRunes().Sum(GetColumns) with string.GetColumns() for
grapheme-aware width measurement. The rune-sum pattern produces incorrect
(inflated) results for multi-rune grapheme clusters like ZWJ emoji
sequences -- e.g. a family emoji measures 8 columns by rune sum but
occupies only 2 terminal cells.

Also fix Branch.GetWidth() which used string.Length instead of
GetColumns(), under-counting width for wide CJK characters.

Fixed instances:
- AppendAutocomplete.cs: autocomplete fragment width
- ColorBar.cs: color label offset (removed obsolete TODO comment)
- FileDialog.cs: feedback text centering
- TabView.cs: tab header width
- TableView.cs (4 instances): column auto-sizing, truncation, padding
- Branch.cs: line truncation check + GetWidth() calculation

Added tests:
- StringTests: 7 parameterized cases documenting rune-sum vs GetColumns
  discrepancy for ZWJ emoji, CJK, combining marks, and ASCII
- TableViewTests: grapheme cluster width comparison + rendering test
- TabViewTests: rendering test verifying emoji tab header width
- TreeViewTests: rendering test with CJK wide characters

Fixes #4798

commit.message:
Replace python-gnupg with subprocess for GPG 2.4.x compatibility

python-gnupg 2.3.1 is incompatible with GPG 2.4.x: version string parsing
fails and decrypt_file returns ok=False even with the correct passphrase.
GPG 2.1+ requires --pinentry-mode loopback for non-interactive passphrase
input, which python-gnupg does not pass.

Switch to a direct subprocess.run call with --pinentry-mode loopback and
--passphrase-fd 0. Remove the python-gnupg dependency entirely.

Update tests to mock subprocess.run instead of the gnupg module.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(api): prevent duplicate file uploads in content preview (#108)

The content preview upload endpoint accepted duplicate files without
validation. Two checks are now enforced before processing uploads:

- **Within-batch dedup**: Rejects requests where multiple items share
the same `filename` (400)
- **Cross-request dedup**: Rejects items whose `title` matches existing
content already stored for the target item (400)

```typescript
// Within-batch: duplicate filenames
const filenames = items.map((item) => item.filename);
const uniqueFilenames = new Set(filenames);
if (uniqueFilenames.size !== filenames.length) { ... return 400 }

// Cross-request: title collision with existing content
const existingContents = await getItemContents(itemId);
const existingTitles = new Set(
  existingContents
    .map((c) => (c.data as unknown as Record<string, unknown>)?.title)
    .filter((title): title is string => typeof title === "string")
);
const duplicateWithExisting = items.filter((item) => existingTitles.has(item.title));
if (duplicateWithExisting.length > 0) { ... return 400 }
```

Error responses include the specific duplicate names for debuggability.
Four e2e tests added covering both single and multiple duplicate
scenarios within a batch, and cross-request title collisions.

commit.message:
Replace s[i] and store8(ptr(tmp)) with _byte_to_char

The s[i] subscript operator and store8(ptr(tmp), ch) pattern for
creating 1-char strings both use store8 internally, which has
register clobbering issues on ARM64 with Novus V0.1.3.

Replace with _byte_to_char() which uses a string literal lookup
table (no store8/memory modification) and works reliably on
all platforms.

docs(docker): add nginx + PHP-FPM reference configuration to fix login redirect loop (#8207)

ChurchCRM users deploying with nginx + PHP-FPM hit an infinite redirect
loop (`/session/begin → /session/begin`) because nginx routes everything
to the root `index.php`, which redirects unauthenticated users to
`/session/begin` — which also hits `index.php` — and so on.

The loop happens because ChurchCRM has **9 independent Slim 4 entry
points** (one per subdirectory). Apache resolves this automatically via
per-directory `.htaccess` mod_rewrite rules; nginx requires explicit
`location` blocks per sub-app.

## Changes

- **`docker/nginx/default.conf`** — nginx server block with `location
^~` blocks routing each sub-application prefix to its own `index.php`.
Regex locations for static assets and PHP, security denies for `/logs`,
`/tmp_attach`, and dotfiles. Commented subdirectory-install variant at
the bottom.

```nginx
# Each Slim sub-app gets its own routing block
location ^~ /session {
    try_files $uri /session/index.php$is_args$args;
    location ~ \.php$ {
        fastcgi_pass  php-fpm:9000;
        fastcgi_param SCRIPT_FILENAME $document_root$fastcgi_script_name;
        include       fastcgi_params;
    }
}
# ... repeated for /api, /v2, /admin, /finance, /kiosk, /plugins, /external, /setup
```

- **`docker/Dockerfile.churchcrm-fpm-php8`** — PHP-FPM multi-stage image
(`prod` / `dev`) with all required extensions (`bcmath`, `gd`, `intl`,
`mysqli`, `pdo_mysql`, `sodium`, `zip`, …). Mirrors the existing
`Dockerfile.churchcrm-apache-php8`.

- **`docker/docker-compose.nginx.yaml`** — Self-contained Compose
reference (MariaDB + PHP-FPM + nginx). Not wired into CI; intended as a
starting point for self-hosted deployments.

- **`docker/README.md`** — Added intro table distinguishing dev/CI
(Apache) from self-hosted (nginx), plus a new section explaining the
routing architecture, the loop pitfall, quick-start, and customisation
notes.

fix: replace style() element with CSS class in locked-by-other card

style("...") in ZIO HTTP template2 creates a <style> HTML element,
not an inline style attribute. This injected a bare <style> tag into
the patchElements SSE payload, which Datastar extracts and processes
before passing to idiomorph — corrupting the HTML tree and preventing
the element card morph from applying in other clients' browsers.

Fix: add .lock-note CSS rule to globalCss and use className := "lock-note"
on the locked-by-other actions span. This resolves the bug where
Alice locking an element would not update Bob's UI in real time even
when both clients had the page detail loaded.

fix(ac-skills): require code_read after code_write for adversarial evidence

Adversarial L1 reviewer only sees tool evidence (first 400 chars of result).
When agents write large files via code_write, the reviewer can't verify the
content and flags HALLUCINATION/TOOL_OUTPUT_INCOMPLETE.

Fix: mandate code_read after every code_write so the content is visible in
the tool evidence chain and the adversarial reviewer can validate the work.

Affects: ac-architect (INCEPTION.md writes) and ac-codex (TDD code writes).

commit.message:
Merge pull request #11 from dchu3/fix/telegram-confirmation-deadlock

fix: resolve confirmation deadlock in Telegram bot

grammY's bot.start() processes updates sequentially — the message
handler was awaiting runAgent(), which awaited the confirmFn Promise
(waiting for an inline keyboard tap). But the button tap is a callback
query update that grammY couldn't process until the message handler
returned, creating a deadlock that always timed out.

Fix: Run agent processing as a fire-and-forget async task so the
message handler returns immediately, allowing grammY to process
callback query updates concurrently.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: security hardening — path traversal, log leaks, race condition, error exposure

Tier 1 security fixes from TECH_DEBT_AUDIT_2026Q1:

- Path traversal (3 files): Replace inline canonicalize checks with
  PathSecurityUtils.validateExtractionPath() for defense-in-depth
- Sensitive log leaks (5 files): Wrap debugPrint/logDebug of UDP messages,
  device fingerprints, AI request bodies, clipboard content, SQL queries
  with kDebugMode guards
- Race condition: Rewrite _executeWithLock to use for(;;) with re-check
  after each await, preventing multiple waiters from acquiring the lock
- Error info leaks (5 files): Guard e.toString() in user-facing SnackBars
  and EmergencyApp with kDebugMode to hide internals in release builds
